findings.claim: The addin's type classifier uses `exists/get(nm, inherits = TRUE)` so a bare highlighted name resolves through the search path — `mpg` resolves to ggplot2's `mpg` dataset (a data.frame) and is classified "dataframe" (promoting ppUpload), a deliberately-accepted false-positive since every placeholder still appears in the list.
findings.detail: The picker order is then `ptr_addin_order(pickable, kind)`: tier-1 promotion by kind, tier-2 by role (consumer<value<source<structural), tier-3 built-ins before customs, tier-4 alphabetical — so a custom placeholder interleaves with its role group instead of sinking to the bottom.
findings.verification: R/paintr-addin.R:90-91 ptr_addin_classify uses exists/get(inherits=TRUE); ptr_addin_order tiers (promotion->role->builtin->alpha) confirmed by tests (a custom value/consumer interleaves by role).

findings.claim: `rstudioapi::getThemeInfo()` returns a list with `$dark` (logical, TRUE when the editor theme is dark), usable to auto-theme a Shiny gadget to match the IDE; it returns NULL/errors outside RStudio so wrap in `tryCatch` with a fallback.
findings.verification: rstudioapi getThemeInfo.Rd documents $dark (logical); R/paintr-addin.R:169-176 ptr_addin_dark_mode reads option override then getThemeInfo()$dark with tryCatch fallback.

findings.claim: miniUI's `miniTitleBar` calls `tagAppendAttributes(right, class = "pull-right")` on its `right` (and `left`) slot, so that slot must be a SINGLE tag — passing a `shiny::tagList(...)` mis-applies the attribute and leaks the literal text "pull-right" into the rendered bar while breaking button float.
findings.detail: Fix for multiple title-bar buttons is to wrap them in one `shiny::div(...)`. Discovered by reading `print(miniUI:::miniTitleBar)`: body is `tags$div(class="gadget-title", tags$h1(title), tagAppendAttributes(left,...), tagAppendAttributes(right,...))`. Also: the gadget `<h1>` title duplicates and visually collides with the dialogViewer window title — set the gadget title to "" when using `dialogViewer()`.
findings.verification: miniUI:::miniTitleBar body contains tagAppendAttributes(right, 'pull-right') (grep TRUE); R/paintr-addin.R:260 uses `right = shiny::div(...)`. Behaviour (tagList leaks text) observed in the user's screenshot and fixed.

findings.claim: The five built-in placeholder keywords `ppVar`/`ppNum`/`ppText`/`ppExpr`/`ppUpload` (plus structural `ppLayerOff`/`ppVerbSwitch`) are exported from the package namespace, and the first four are identity functions, so a "naked" ggplot expression using them (e.g. `ggplot(iris, aes(x = ppVar(Sepal.Length))) + geom_point(size = ppNum(3))`) evaluates as ordinary ggplot2 outside `ptr_app()`.
findings.detail: Verified by `ggplot_build()` on such an expression -> 150-row layer. `ppUpload(x)` is identity with an arg but no-arg `ppUpload()` aborts outside `ptr_app()`. This is what makes a README chunk runnable with `eval=TRUE` without launching Shiny.
findings.verification: NAMESPACE exports all 5 pp* (grep=5); ggplot_build on a naked ppVar/ppNum expression yielded a 150-row layer this session.

findings.claim: `ptr_capture_formula` resolves a bare symbol bound to a stored formula: when the enexpr-captured arg is a symbol it is `rlang::eval_bare(sym, envir)`-resolved and accepted if the value is a string/call/symbol, so `formula_iris <- rlang::expr(ggplot(...)); ptr_app(formula_iris)` works WITHOUT `!!`.
findings.detail: Verified by probe: `ptr_capture_formula(rlang::expr(formula_iris), environment())` returned the deparsed ggplot string. The `!!` form is only required for non-symbol captures (e.g. subscripted `plots[[1]]`). CLAUDE.md's "splice stored exprs with !!" is one valid path but not the only one — bare stored symbols resolve too (documented "f <- '...'; ptr_app(f)" contract extends to call-valued symbols).
findings.verification: R/paintr-app.R:210 is_symbol branch + :221 eval_bare resolve; probe ptr_capture_formula(expr(formula_iris),env) returned the deparsed ggplot string.

findings.claim: R CMD check's "non-ASCII characters" WARNING fires on non-ASCII in R *code* (string literals, symbols) but NOT in comments/roxygen; fix with `\uXXXX` escapes or ASCII equivalents (curly quotes, `…`, `↑↓` in gadget strings triggered it).
findings.detail: `tools::showNonASCIIfile("R/x.R")` lists every non-ASCII line (over-reports comments). Only the string-literal lines matter for the warning; making the whole file ASCII via a `perl -CSD -i -pe 's/\x{2026}/.../g; ...'` pass guarantees it clears and is verifiable by re-running showNonASCIIfile (empty output).
findings.source: R CMD check on ggpaintr 0.10.0; tools::showNonASCIIfile
findings.verification: Empirical: R CMD check raised the non-ASCII WARNING citing R/paintr-addin.R while curly-quote/ellipsis/arrow chars were in string literals; after ASCII-izing, tools::showNonASCIIfile returns empty and check is clean.

findings.claim: Rendering a `github_document` README that references an image path which does not yet exist fails with "pandoc ... error 99" (missing resource); the referenced PNG must exist before knitting. A `ptr_app()` example chunk must be a display-only ```r block (not ```{r}) because evaluating it launches a Shiny session and hangs the knit.
findings.detail: For generated plot figures, set `knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.path = "man/figures/README-")` so they land in the conventional dir referenced by README.md and shipped to pkgdown.
findings.verification: Empirical: render errored 'pandoc ... error 99' until man/figures/ggpaintr-addin.png existed; ptr_app() chunks left as ```r (executing them launches Shiny). README.Rmd:13 sets fig.path=man/figures/README-.

findings.claim: An RStudio addin package CANNOT ship a default keyboard shortcut — bindings live only in the user's `~/.config/rstudio/keybindings/addins.json` (macOS/Linux ≥1.3) or `~/AppData/Roaming/RStudio/keybindings/addins.json` (Windows), keyed by command-id `package::function`; and an addin appears in the shortcut dialog only after the package is INSTALLED, not merely `devtools::load_all()`-ed.
findings.detail: `addins.dcf` has no shortcut field. Bind via Tools > Addins > Browse Addins... > Keyboard Shortcuts..., or Tools > Modify Keyboard Shortcuts. The exact macOS Command-key token in that JSON could not be authoritatively confirmed from docs (Cmd vs Command vs Meta) — read it back from a binding set in the GUI if a writer is ever built.
findings.source: Posit docs; shrtcts/rstudio.prefs pkg docs; inst/rstudio/addins.dcf
findings.verification: inst/rstudio/addins.dcf has no Shortcut field (grep=0). Path/keying/install-only-in-dialog from Posit + shrtcts/rstudio.prefs docs this session. SUB-DETAIL UNCONFIRMED: exact macOS Command-key token in addins.json (Cmd vs Command vs Meta) — read back from a GUI-set binding if ever writing that file.

findings.claim: Rich per-row rendering in a `selectizeInput` uses `choices = NULL` plus `options = list(options = <unnamed list of dicts>, valueField, labelField, searchField, render = I("{ option: fn, item: fn }"))` — and the render marker is base `I()`, not `shiny::I` (which does not exist).
findings.detail: Each dict supplies the fields the JS render reads (e.g. kw/role/desc); the JS `escape()` is selectize-provided. `htmltools::renderTags(ui)$head` (NOT `$html`) is where a `tags$head(tags$style(...))` block lands when verifying CSS embedding headlessly.
findings.verification: R/paintr-addin.R:226 ptr_addin_render_js uses base I(r"(...)"); run_palette passes choices=NULL + options(options=, valueField/labelField/searchField, render=). shiny::I errored ('not an exported object') this session, confirming base I().

findings.claim: The "Listening on http://127.0.0.1:<port>" line printed when a Shiny gadget runs is emitted by `shiny:::startApp` via `message()`, so wrapping `shiny::runGadget(...)` in `suppressMessages()` silences it cleanly.
findings.detail: Confirmed by grepping the shiny namespace: only `startApp` contains the "Listening on" string, via `message("\n","Listening on http://",...)`. A gadget is a real local Shiny app, hence the line.
findings.verification: Only shiny:::startApp contains 'Listening on', emitted via message(); R/paintr-addin.R:308 wraps runGadget in suppressMessages().
